Title: An extremely promiscuous terpenoid synthase from the Lamiaceae plant Colquhounia coccinea var. mollis catalyzes the formation of sester-/di-/sesqui-/mono-terpenoids

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Colquhounia coccinea var. mollis; Escherichia coli; Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Colquhounia coccinea var. mollis; Escherichia coli; Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Terpene synthase, TPS-b subfamily; contains DDXXD, DTE, and RR(X)8W motifs
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves of Colquhounia coccinea var. mollis; transiently expressed Nicotiana benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Likely involved in plant–environment interactions and responses to abiotic stress; no obvious antifeedant activity against Helicoverpa armigera was detected for compounds 3–7
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly leaf-specific; predominant in leaves, low in epidermis-free tender stems and flowers; induced by methyl jasmonate at 3 h
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In C. coccinea var. mollis, leaf-enriched and MeJA inducible; in N. benthamiana transient expression produced detectable terpenoids
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter for CcTPS1::GFP localization; pEAQ-HT high-level transient expression system in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Methyl jasmonate treatment induced CcTPS1 expression and accumulation of compounds 3 and 7
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Geranylfarnesyl diphosphate (GFDP); geranylgeranyl diphosphate (GGDP); farnesyl diphosphate (FDP); geranyl diphosphate (GDP)
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes multiple linear prenyl diphosphate substrates (C25/C20/C15/C10) into diverse terpenoid hydrocarbon and alcohol scaffolds via carbocation-mediated cyclization
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; proposed to be mainly responsible for diterpenoid and sesquiterpenoid biosynthesis in planta
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Leaf transcriptome mining; full-length cDNA cloning; heterologous coexpression in engineered E. coli; in vitro enzyme assays with GDP/FDP/GGDP; metabolite profiling by GC–MS; purification and NMR structural elucidation of products; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; GFP localization; MeJA induction; site-directed mutagenesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes formation of multiple sester-/di-/sesqui-/mono-terpenoids; mainly associated with diterpenoid 3 and sesquiterpenoid 7 accumulation in planta
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CcTPS1 is the first plant TPS reported to accept GFDP, GGDP, FDP, and GDP and produce sester-, di-, sesqui-, and mono-terpenoids. 2. It generated eight characterized products, including two new sesterterpenoids and one new diterpenoid, in engineered E. coli, and produced compounds 3–7 in Nicotiana benthamiana. 3. CcTPS1 is cytosolic, highly leaf-specific, and inducible by methyl jasmonate, supporting a role in plant environmental responses. 4. Detection of diterpenoid 3 and sesquiterpenoid 7 in leaves suggests CcTPS1 mainly contributes to diterpenoid and sesquiterpenoid biosynthesis in planta.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Leaf and epidermis-free tender stem transcriptome sequencing using Illumina HiSeq 2000; de novo assembly; unigene annotation against Nr, Swiss-Prot, COG, and KEGG
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Extreme promiscuity may cause broad product mixtures; only some products were detected in planta, indicating context-dependent flux and possible unintended metabolic diversification in engineering applications
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for metabolic engineering to generate diverse rare terpenoid scaffolds, especially cyclohexane-containing sesterterpenoids and diterpenoids; promising enzyme for transcriptome mining-guided natural product discovery and biocatalyst design
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although it carries an RR(X)8W motif typical of mono-TPSs, CcTPS1 lacks a plastid transit peptide and is cytosolic. In planta, diterpenoid 3 was the major product, whereas sesquiterpenoids predominated in E. coli, indicating host-dependent product profiles.
